milestone: MLA-1 (C5 active-path -> L3; after the MLA-3 YaRN mscale fix)
ladder_level_whole_model: L3 (C1+C2+C5 real-weight active path; C3+C4 mechanism)
headline: MoE-certified L3 (whole model) = L2 + C5 (active-path-certified)
headline_note: Per ADR-007 the bare word 'certified' is forbidden for MoE. This family is 'MoE-certified L3' = L2 + C5 (active-path-certified). L1: C1 (per-expert) exhaustive over ALL 26 MoE layers x 64 routed experts = 1664 experts + C2 (router top-6 set equality) on all 26 MoE layers, both on the REAL trained weights vs a float64 reference computed one expert at a time; C3 (MLA attention) via the mechanism cert. C4 (assembly/topology): the MLA-0 V2-Lite-topology full-forward cert vs HF f64 = 9.072e-5. C5 (active-path): the REAL DeepSeek-V2-Lite, as actually run end-to-end on a canonical input via the MLA-2 disk expert-tier, vs a float64 HF reference computed ONE DECODER LAYER AT A TIME = whole-model max_abs_diff 2.587e-5 (< 0.5), per-position argmax exact 4/4, deterministic. This required the MLA-3 fix (YaRN attention_scaling, NOT mscale^2 on the softmax scale). This is L3, NOT the dense ADR-004 CERTIFIED, and NOT L4 (global model.double() F64, reserved/unreachable).

C1_per_expert.scope: ALL 26 MoE layers (layers 1-26; dense layer 0 skipped) x 64 routed experts = 1664 experts (exhaustive)
C1_per_expert.weights: real trained DeepSeek-V2-Lite weights (bf16; 3 MoE layers span two shards)
C1_per_expert.oracle: NumPy/torch float64, one expert at a time, one MoE layer at a time (no full-model F64)
C1_per_expert.metric: max_abs_diff(Atenia expert forward, F64 reference) per expert
C1_per_expert.gate: max_abs_diff < 0.5 for EVERY expert (ADR-004 bar, unchanged)
C1_per_expert.result: all 1664 experts < 0.5 (global worst ~1.9e-6, ~2.6e5x inside the gate)
C1_per_expert.shared_expert_note: the 2 always-on ungated shared experts are part of the MoE block convention (C3/mechanism); C1 here covers the 64 routed experts per layer.

C2_router.hard_gate: top-6 expert SET EQUALITY vs the F64 reference, every MoE layer
C2_router.routing_margin_note: gap between the 6th and 7th router logit per layer. norm_topk_prob=false (no renorm) does not affect the top-k SET (softmax monotonic); the tightest layer (23, margin 0.0120) still matched exactly.
C2_router.result: top-6 set identical on all 26 MoE layers

C3_attention.evidence: DeepSeek MLA attention cert (layer-0, 9.999e-06 vs HF, MOE-FULL-12) + MLA-0 V2-Lite-like full forward (9.072e-05 vs HF, YaRN + dense-first)
C3_attention.caveat: certifies the MLA attention + YaRN MECHANISM (tiny fixtures), NOT the real-weight per-layer attention re-certified. Same standard as Qwen-MoE C3. Recorded, not over-claimed.

C4_assembly_topology.primary_topology: a tiny real DeepseekV2ForCausalLM end-to-end with the EXACT V2-Lite conventions: YaRN (factor8, mscale0.707) + first_k_dense_replace=1 (dense layer 0) + norm_topk_prob=false (no-renorm, ungated shared) + MLA + q_lora_rank=null, at reduced hidden dim
C4_assembly_topology.secondary_evidence: deepseek_scale topology cert 7.806e-3 (16-routed/top-6/2-shared/MLA, MOE-FULL-15, tests/moe_scale_cert_test.rs::deepseek_scale_topology_certifies; f32-vs-f64, argmax exact) -- a different DeepSeek-MoE topology point (norm_topk_prob=true, no dense-first/YaRN)
C4_assembly_topology.gate: end-to-end max_abs_diff < 0.5 (ADR-004 bar, unchanged); both clear it (MLA-0 also clears its 5e-3 test bound, deepseek_scale its 1e-2 bound)
C4_assembly_topology.result: PASS -- the routing+combine+assembly+MLA+YaRN+dense-first mechanism is certified end-to-end vs HF f64 on the V2-Lite topology (9.072e-5) + a DeepSeek-MoE scale point (7.806e-3)
C4_assembly_topology.caveat: C4 certifies the MECHANISM on reduced-dim, RANDOM-weight topology fixtures -- NOT the real 64-expert trained-weight assembly. Per ADR-007 it transfers to the real model only IN CONJUNCTION WITH the whole-model C1/C2 (real weights), which are met. Recorded, not over-claimed.

C5_active_path.certified_in: MLA-1 (C5), after the MLA-3 YaRN mscale fix
C5_active_path.scope: the REAL DeepSeek-V2-Lite, as actually run end-to-end (MLA + YaRN + dense-first + MoE), full forward through the controlled MoeRuntime on the MLA-2 disk expert-tier (~4 GB RAM)
C5_active_path.oracle: HuggingFace DeepseekV2DecoderLayer in float64, ONE DECODER LAYER AT A TIME (never the whole model in F64 -> NOT L4); driver validated against the tiny MLA-0 fixture (8.126e-9 vs the full-model HF f64) before the real run
C5_active_path.metric: whole-model logits max_abs_diff(Atenia disk-tier forward, F64 reference) + per-position argmax
C5_active_path.gate: end-to-end max_abs_diff < 0.5 (ADR-004 bar, unchanged) + exact per-position argmax
C5_active_path.timing: load ~1299 s (writes the ephemeral f32 expert tier to NVMe) + forward ~228 s
C5_active_path.result: PASS -- 2.587e-5 < 0.5 (the f32-precision floor; the HF f32-vs-f64 control on this model is 3.111e-5 with identical argmax), argmax exact 4/4, deterministic. Real-weight MLA attention is now validated end-to-end (supersedes the C3 mechanism caveat for the active path).
C5_active_path.note: Required the MLA-3 fix: YaRN attention_scaling = get_mscale(factor,mscale)/get_mscale(factor,mscale_all_dim) (=1.0 for V2-Lite) folded into q_pe/k_pe, NOT mscale^2 on the softmax scale. Before the fix C5 was 2.032 (argmax 3/4 FAIL); see docs/HANDOFF_MLA1_C5_ROOT_CAUSE.md + docs/HANDOFF_MLA_3.md.

pending_levels.blocker: global model.double() F64 ~126 GB — reserved / unreachable; never fabricated.

remaining_caveats: C3 attention is still recorded at the mechanism level (tiny fixtures + MLA-0); however C5 now validates the real-weight MLA attention end-to-end through the full active path.
C1/C2 use a single deterministic probe input applied to every MoE layer's real weights (representative activation, not a real inter-layer trace) — consistent with the Qwen approach.
C5 uses one canonical input ([1,100,200,300], seq 4); other inputs are not separately certified (same single-probe standard as Qwen-MoE C5).
C4 (and the C3 fixture) are certified at the MECHANISM level (reduced-dim, random-weight topology fixtures); the real-weight assembly is covered by C1/C2 (pieces) + C5 (active path).
L3 here is the active-path certification; it is NOT the dense ADR-004 CERTIFIED, and NOT L4 (global F64, reserved/unreachable).

verification.c1_c2_reference: fixtures/moe/deepseek_v2lite_decomp_ref.safetensors (+ .json), float64 one expert at a time (~13.6 MB)
verification.c1_c2_expected: C1 global worst max_abs_diff ~1.9e-6 (< 0.5, all 1664 experts, layer 26 / expert 57); C2 top-6 set match on all 26 MoE layers; min routing margin 0.011981 (layer 23); 0 failures. Real run ~505 s.
verification.c4_primary_command: cargo test --release --test moe_mla0_deepseek_v2lite_test mla0_v2lite_full_forward_matches_hf -- --nocapture  (expected end-to-end max_abs_diff 5.306e-5 after MLA-3, argmax exact)
verification.c4_secondary_command: cargo test --release --test moe_scale_cert_test deepseek_scale_topology_certifies -- --nocapture  (expected 7.806e-3, argmax exact)
verification.c5_reference: fixtures/moe/deepseek_v2lite_c5_ref.safetensors (+ .json), float64 one decoder layer at a time; generator fixtures/moe/generate_deepseek_v2lite_c5_reference.py (modes tiny/real/diag/precision)
verification.c5_command: python fixtures/moe/generate_deepseek_v2lite_c5_reference.py real models/DeepSeek-V2-Lite fixtures/moe ; then ATENIA_DISK_TIER_DIR=<nvme> DEEPSEEK_V2_LITE_DIR=models/DeepSeek-V2-Lite cargo test --release --test moe_mla1_deepseek_c5_active_path_test -- --ignored mla1_real_deepseek_v2lite_c5_active_path --nocapture
verification.c5_expected: whole-model max_abs_diff ~2.587e-5 (< 0.5), per-position argmax exact 4/4, deterministic. Real run ~1299 s load + ~228 s forward, ~4 GB RAM (disk tier).

generated_on: F64 references: torch (bf16->float64) + NumPy float64, Windows 11. Atenia: CPU forward (C5 via MLA-2 disk expert-tier), RTX 4070 Laptop host, 32 GB RAM.
